items.source: Salesforce activity ground truth (buyer email received)

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated concrete pain: roughly 70% of ACEN's spend is non-PO (loan/tax payments) and manually disconnected from their in-house P2P system (Zeeshan Pervez, 28 Feb 2025 call, ~17 months ago). The formal decision weighting explicitly splits value 50% functional fit / 50% tech capability and ROI (Jennelyn Nicolas, 7 Apr 2025 note, ~15 months ago), but no updated quantified business case has been confirmed since the Oct 2025 request to re-scope for budget.

champion.narrative: Mher Tolentino-Blas (Head of Procurement, referred to as the CPO in Next Step notes) was flagged as the Zycus champion following a "strong F2F meeting" on 13 Feb 2025 (~17 months ago), citing momentum from the sister-company GCash close. No confirmed direct engagement with her is evidenced after mid-2025, and with roughly ten months of quiet since the Sep 2025 postponement, champion strength has weakened and is at risk absent fresh confirmation.

competition.narrative: ACEN confirmed Zycus as one of the final two vendors on 3 Jun 2025 (~13 months ago), with SAP Ariba named as the other finalist. A live commercial threat tied to this — SAP's secondary integration/connection fee — was flagged as a genuine risk to award timing on 22 May and 29 May 2025, and was still under buyer-side negotiation with SAP as of 23 Jun 2025 (~12.5 months ago), with no confirmed resolution since. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua appear in the Competitors__c field but were not referenced after the top-two down-select and are treated as faded.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-documented and buyer-articulated: a homegrown in-house P2P system with no vendor brand, manual supplier onboarding via email plus third-party verification, ad hoc purchases and spend-analysis gaps, and a bespoke non-PO "request for payment" module the buyer's own team struggled to explain internally (21 Feb 2025 demo notes and 28 Feb 2025 call, both ~17 months ago).

paper_process.narrative: On 13 Aug 2025 (~11 months ago), Zycus proactively sent the Order Form and MSA to Jeff De Sagun for preliminary legal review to pre-empt later delays ("I wanted to send you the copy of order form, MSA... since we are gonna do few things in parallel"). No confirmed redline or legal sign-off status exists in any subsequent evidence.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Zeeshan Pervez, Decision Maker; Jonathan Back, Executive Sponsor)
economic_buyer.narrative: Zeeshan Pervez (President, ACEN Shared Services) is the confirmed Decision Maker in Salesforce and has personally driven exco advancement throughout: "I'm part of that management team that's gonna make that decision... there are 4 people in the company who's gonna make this decision... my boss, he's completely aligned" (28 Feb 2025, ~17 months ago). Jonathan Back (CFO and Chief Strategy Officer) is tagged Executive Sponsor and was described as actively backing Zycus (27 Feb 2025 note). Direct engagement with either has not been confirmed since roughly Sep 2025 (~10 months ago); the 7 Jul 2026 buyer email reply (2 days ago) is the first sign of possible reactivation.

decision_process.narrative: The process runs through an internal assessment/scoring exercise, then presentation to the IT steering committee, then exco sign-off. As of the 13 Aug 2025 call (~11 months ago), Jeff De Sagun (Senior IT Application Specialist) could not confirm the steering-committee timing ("we don't know yet"). Exco decisions have since slipped repeatedly: postponed a quarter on 22 Sep 2025, and again pending a budget-fit resubmission from 14 Oct 2025 — neither has a confirmed outcome on record.

decision_criteria.narrative: Jennelyn Nicolas (Senior Manager, IT) responded with a formal selection criteria table weighting 50% functional fit and 50% tech capability plus ROI (7 Apr 2025 note, ~15 months ago). This criteria set has not been revisited or updated in any evidence since.

items.summary: Carl Kimball (Zycus VP APAC) reached out to Zee after a gap in contact, inviting him and colleagues to Zycus' "Horizon" event in Singapore (21-22 July), offering waived fees and a possible speaking slot; this is relationship/brand touchpoint rather than deal progression, and a reply is pending on whether Zee wants to attend.

day_summary.overall: The only activity today was an inbound invite from Zycus' APAC VP inviting Zee to their July industry event in Singapore, positioned as a networking/thought-leadership forum rather than a sales pitch. It's a relationship-nurturing outreach after a period of quiet on the account, not a substantive deal move — the ball is now in Zee's court to respond if interested.

cro_panel.intro: This is a deeply qualified but long-stalled deal; a delivered on-site POC, a confirmed top-two finalist position against SAP Ariba, and named C-level backing all happened in the first half of 2025, but the deal has been effectively quiet since the 22 Sep 2025 exco postponement.; A genuine buyer-side email reply on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days ago) is the first real reactivation signal in roughly ten months and should be treated as live, not dead; but nothing concrete has been re-confirmed yet

critical_signals.text: After roughly ten months of largely rep-side outreach following the September 2025 exco postponement, the buyer replied by email on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days ago), the first confirmed two-way touch since the quiet stretch began; the rep followed up again today.

SAP Ariba is the only confirmed live rival, named the other finalist over a year ago (3 Jun 2025); the SAP secondary integration cost objection that could tip the decision toward the incumbent ecosystem was never confirmed resolved.
The budget-trimmed, exco-ready proposal Zeeshan Pervez asked for on 14 Oct 2025 to get the deal through committee has no confirmed delivery or exco outcome on record nine months later.
No new stakeholder has surfaced since mid-2025; engagement remains effectively single-threaded through Zeeshan Pervez, with the CFO and CEO named as sponsors but not directly engaged in over a year.

champion_strength.summary: Mher Tolentino-Blas (Head of Procurement) was flagged as Zycus's champion following a strong F2F meeting on 13 Feb 2025 (~17 months ago), citing momentum from the sister-company GCash close. No confirmed direct engagement with her is evidenced past early 2025, and the roughly ten-month quiet stretch since the Sep 2025 postponement puts champion strength at risk.

expansion_context.note: ACEN leadership referenced a sister/affiliate company (GCash) as an existing successful Zycus customer multiple times (4 Dec 2024 and 13 Feb 2025 notes; also referenced live in the 28 Feb 2025 call transcript: "the way we did for Gcash") as internal social proof. This is not a direct ACEN sibling opportunity in this record, but indicates a warm within-group reputation worth leveraging in re-engagement.

No confirmed direct buyer meeting in roughly ten months — the last documented push was Zeeshan Pervez's early-Sep 2025 meeting with Carl Kimball, after which the project was postponed by exco on 22 Sep 2025.
The SAP secondary integration cost objection, flagged 22 May 2025 as a real threat to award timing, was never confirmed resolved, leaving SAP Ariba's relative cost position unclear.

A genuine two-way buyer touch just landed — an incoming email reply on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days ago) is the first confirmed buyer response since the extended quiet period, with the rep following up again today, 9 Jul 2026.
